items.title: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
items.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes kill 920 people as international rescue teams arrive
items.summary: Hundreds are still feared trapped under the rubble, as families desperate for news.

items.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
items.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

items.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
items.summary: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

items.title: Europe's deadly heatwave breaks German record and halts public events
items.summary: Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C is recorded provisionally in Saarbrücken, over the border from France.

items.title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
items.summary: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.

items.title: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
items.summary: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
items.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

items.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
items.summary: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

items.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
items.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

items.title: Senior Ukrainian intelligence official jailed for life for spying for Russia
items.summary: Col Dmytro Kozyura agreed to share state secrets and systematically disclosed classified information, prosecutors say.

items.title: Four men held over child marriage in Sierra Leone appear in landmark court case
items.summary: It relates to a 17-year-old girl - four men including her father and husband face years in jail if convicted.

items.title: Canada eligible to join Eurovision competition
items.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney raised the idea of Canada joining the song contest in his 2025 budget.

items.title: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
items.summary: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

items.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
items.summary: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

items.title: They quit the West for Russia's traditional values, but it wasn't what they expected
items.summary: The BBC asks Westerners who moved to Russia if life there lives up to expectations.

items.title: Could you handle a 20-plus hour flight? This airline is banking on it
items.summary: Some analysts say the first non-stop London to Sydney flight is a major milestone - but would you buy a ticket?

items.title: Hit South African show gets the world talking about polygamy and cheating
items.summary: The Zulu-language series about a man with multiple wives is a worldwide hit.

items.title: Why GTA 6 will launch without a disc - and what it means for gamers
items.summary: Music and films are now largely digital-only - does the lack of a disc for GTA 6 mean gaming is going the same way?

items.title: Just how much trouble is Canada's economy in?
items.summary: Five charts help illustrate the state of Canada's economy - and how it's performing compared to other wealthy nations.

items.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
items.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

items.title: The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet
items.summary: Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work?

items.title: Watch: BBC reports from La Guaira, one of Venezuela's worst-hit areas
items.summary: Vanessa Silva visited the coastal state, where rescuers are still working to find survivors under the rubble after twin earthquakes rocked the country.

items.title: Watch: Lightning strikes seen across UK
items.summary: Lightning strikes lit up the sky across the UK during the early hours of Friday as thunderstorms were triggered by the intense heat.

items.title: Debris falls after plane hits Beijing's tallest building
items.summary: Social media footage showed the moment debris from a small aircraft fell to the ground after a crash into Beijing's tallest skyscraper.

items.title: Moment woman pulled from rubble alive after Venezuela earthquake
items.summary: First responders and volunteers pulled a survivor from under the rubble of a collapsed building in La Guaira.

items.title: Aerial footage reveals destruction in coastal Venezuela
items.summary: Multi-storey buildings have collapsed in La Guaira following two major earthquakes on Wednesday.
